canto_title: The Hierarchy of Sin and the Classification of Fraud and Violence

scenes.summary: Dante and Virgil arrive at a rocky ledge near a pit and encounter a grave inscription regarding Pope Anastasius.
Virgil advises a slow descent to allow their senses to adjust to the terrible stench of the abyss.
Virgil begins explaining the structure of the circles and the distinction between malice and violence.
Virgil describes the first ring, which contains those who used force against others, their property, or their lives.
The lecture expands to include those who committed violence against themselves and those who blasphemed against God.
Virgil explains how fraud breaks the bonds of trust and describes the various types of fraudulent sins.
Dante asks why the souls in the marsh are punished in a different manner than those in the city.
Virgil responds by referencing the three types of sinlessness found in Ethics: incontinence, malice, and bestiality.
Dante prays for clarity and asks Virgil to revisit the explanation regarding the sin of usury.
Virgil uses philosophy and the Book of Genesis to explain why usury is a sin against nature and God.
Virgil concludes the lecture and directs Dante's attention to the movement of the stars and the zodiac.
